description:
# .43.G — wire lower_with's else cascade onto matrix

## What changes

`lower_with` (src/ir_lower.rs ~2459) has TWO sequential parts:
  1. The `<-` bindings (sequential thread, NOT a parallel match) —
     unchanged in this ticket. lower_pattern_bind still used per
     binding (subsumed in .H when bind becomes a matrix wrapper).
  2. The else-clauses inside `with_fail_cont` — a multi-clause cascade
     identical in shape to lower_case's clause loop. THIS gets the
     matrix wiring.

So .G is essentially .F applied to the with_fail_cont's interior:
- Same eligibility gate
- Same cont-fn-always-wrapped callback (with's else bodies have the
  same shape as case clause bodies)
- Cascade fallback for ineligible else-clauses

## What stays

- The outer `<-` thread structure
- `with_fail_cont` minting + invocation
- `unmatched_v` plumbing
- join_opt for non-tail position

## What's removed

- Per-else-clause try_blocks cascade INSIDE with_fail_cont (when
  eligible)

## Acceptance

- with-using fixtures: golden byte-identical (most don't have
  matrix-sharable else clauses)
- The one fixture with multi-clause else (if any) tightens
- Three-path parity

## Risk

with currently errors on else-guards (line 2572). v1 keeps that.

## NOT in this ticket

- Matrix-ifying the `<-` binding's single-pattern match. That's a
  single-pattern position, subsumed by .H's 1-row matrix wrapper.
title: fz-ul4.43.G: wire lower_with's pattern step onto matrix
